tactical_shape: Won a series of corners and positioned their taller players at the back of the box on corners
Used a high-energy attacking approach with Ødegaard carrying play and Haaland attacking in behind
standout_players: Erling Haaland — scored twice, taking his World Cup tally to four; described as a 'phenomenon' and executioner-like finisher
Martin Ødegaard — orchestrated attacks, including the pass for Haaland's second
Marcus Holmgren Pedersen — came on for the injured Ryerson and scored after Koulibaly's mistake
Antonio Nusa — curled the diagonal cross for the chance that led to Ødegaard's saved volley
Patrick Berg — cut the ball back for Haaland's second
fatigue_injuries: Julian Ryerson limped off on 12 minutes with what looked like a muscular injury
home_read.momentum: Celebratory and buoyant: Norway have qualified for the knockout phase for the first time since France 1998 and look like a strong, well-rounded side with Haaland at the centre of it.

tactical_shape: Pressed on the right for a while, but without real precision
Played a high-risk, error-prone game at the back under Norway's pressure
standout_players: Nicolas Jackson — lively and mobile, but usually offside
Sadio Mané — involved in Senegal's goal, easing the ball into Sarr's path
Kalidou Koulibaly — had a nightmare, making mistakes that contributed to all three Norway goals
away_read.momentum: Discouraging and shaky: after two defeats and six goals conceded, Senegal look badly exposed and error-prone despite briefly rallying to score.
